player_utterance: You've been quiet a long time, John. I've been watching you sit with whatever it is you're carrying, and I think we both know it's time to stop pretending it isn't there.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, funeral program folded into quarters in one hand, paper cup in the other

player_utterance: I was at the service this morning.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, unfolding the program a quarter-turn and folding it back

player_utterance: I noticed you didn't say amen.

believer_state_after.posture: lowering himself onto the bench, program still in hand, paper cup set down on the table beside the candle

player_utterance: You couldn't say it, could you. Not wouldn't — couldn't.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program flat on his knee now, both hands resting on it

voice_offers.read: He noticed himself not saying it. Name the unbelief.
voice_offers.line: You couldn't say it, could you. Not wouldn't — couldn't.

voice_offers.read: He just sat down. Let the noticing land.
voice_offers.line: I wasn't trying to catch you out. I just saw it.

voice_offers.read: Amen means 'so be it.' Ask what he couldn't affirm.
voice_offers.line: Amen just means 'so be it.' Which part couldn't you agree to?

player_utterance: Which word stopped in your throat? The "so" or the "be it"?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, looking at his hands, not at you

voice_offers.read: He's quiet because the hit landed; press the exact word.
voice_offers.line: Which word stopped in your throat? The 'so' or the 'be it'?

voice_offers.read: He's not fleeing; let him have the bench, the program, the quiet.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to answer that. I'll sit a minute, if that's alright.

player_utterance: You've buried people with that word. When did it stop meaning anything?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, leaning forward now, elbows on his knees, looking at the ground between his shoes

voice_offers.read: He's looking at his hands. The silence is the answer; name it.
voice_offers.line: You've buried people with that word. When did it stop meaning anything?

voice_offers.read: He's not fleeing. He's sitting with it. Let him.
voice_offers.line: I'll wait. Take your time.

voice_offers.read: Press the teacher in him — he answers to texts.
voice_offers.line: You teach the Small Catechism. Luther says amen means 'yes, yes, it shall be so.' Can you still teach that line?

player_utterance: Whose funeral was it? The one where the word died in your mouth.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, leaning forward, the funeral program in both hands now, looking at the photograph on its front

voice_offers.read: He's bent; one more press and the quote-armor cracks.
voice_offers.line: Whose funeral was it? The one where the word died in your mouth.

voice_offers.read: He's already answering in his body; words would trample it.
voice_offers.line: Take whatever time you need. I'm not going anywhere.

voice_offers.read: Pin him to his own classroom, not his grief.
voice_offers.line: You teach children to say it every morning. What do you tell them it means now?

player_utterance: It's them, isn't it. The one on the program. Say their name.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, both thumbs pressed against the edges of the program, eyes on the photograph

voice_offers.read: He's looking at the photo; the name is right there.
voice_offers.line: It's them, isn't it. The one on the program. Say their name.

voice_offers.read: Three cuts in a row; he's holding the picture like it's bones.
voice_offers.line: That's a good picture of them. Who took it?

voice_offers.read: He hides in liturgy; pin him to his own creed instead.
voice_offers.line: Your catechism says amen means God's promise is certain. Was it certain that day?

player_utterance: Say it. Out loud. You owe them that much.

believer_state_after.posture: standing now, slowly, the program falling from his knee to the flagstones, one hand braced on the back of the bench

voice_offers.read: Three cuts landed; he's silent on the name. Drive once more.
voice_offers.line: Say it. Out loud. You owe them that much.

voice_offers.read: He's already there, thumbs white on the program. Words would trample him.

believer_state_after.posture: head in hands on the bench, very still

voice_offers.read: He's standing; one more word finishes it.
voice_offers.line: The name. Now.

voice_offers.read: He rose; the program fell. Meet the body, not the wound.
voice_offers.line: Sit back down. Please. The program — let me get it.

player_utterance: Whisper it, then. Just to me. Just once.

believer_state_after.posture: head still in his hands on the bench, then lifting slowly to look at you

voice_offers.read: He's gone silent and folded; the name won't come by force.
voice_offers.line: Okay. Okay. I'll stop. I'll sit with you.

voice_offers.read: Three refusals — one more push and the dam breaks.
voice_offers.line: Whisper it, then. Just to me. Just once.

player_utterance: I'm right here. No one else. Just the name.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, very still, hands folded on the program in his lap

voice_offers.read: He's lifting his head; one more nudge ends it.
voice_offers.line: I'm right here. No one else. Just the name.

voice_offers.read: He's looking at me — that's the moment to stop pushing.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to. I see you. I'm not going anywhere.

player_utterance: You've been carrying it alone for so long. Set it down. Here. With me.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, very still, hands folded on the program, looking at the candle rather than at you

voice_offers.read: He has nothing left to give and we keep taking.
Three refusals; one more push and the name comes loose.
voice_offers.line: You've been carrying it alone for so long. Set it down. Here. With me.

voice_offers.read: He folds the program — that's the thread, not the name.
voice_offers.line: Whose name is printed in that program, John? Read it to me. Just read what's already there.

player_utterance: One word, John. That's all. One word and it's out of you.

believer_state_after.posture: standing slowly from the bench, the program in his left hand, looking past you toward the door of the house

voice_offers.read: He is silent because he is almost there; let him arrive.
Three asks, no answer; one more push finds the name.
voice_offers.line: One word, John. That's all. One word and it's out of you.

voice_offers.read: The program in his hands holds what his mouth won't.
voice_offers.line: Then open the program. Point to it. You don't have to say anything.

player_utterance: Say it before you walk through that door, or you'll carry it another twenty years.

believer_state_after.posture: standing at the edge of the bench, the program tucked under his arm now, half-turned toward the house

voice_offers.read: He's about to walk; pressing now loses him entirely.
voice_offers.line: Stay. Sit back down. You don't have to say anything — just don't go in there yet.

voice_offers.read: He's standing to flee; the name is right at his teeth.
voice_offers.line: Say it before you walk through that door, or you'll carry it another twenty years.

voice_offers.read: The program in his hand is the artifact; use it.
voice_offers.line: John. The program. Hand it to me.

player_utterance: His name, John. Say his name.

believer_state_after.posture: stopped mid-step on the flagstones, back half-turned, not looking at you

voice_offers.read: He's at the door; one more push and we lose him to shame.
voice_offers.line: John — wait. I'm sorry. Sit. Whatever it is, it doesn't have to be tonight.

voice_offers.read: He's cracking; one more word lands it.
voice_offers.line: His name, John. Say his name.

voice_offers.read: The program under his arm is the artifact; make him show it.
voice_offers.line: The program, John. Under your arm. Give it to me.
